behavior_in_task_description.reason:
Tests assert exact string literals like 'badge={AI}' in Actions.tsx and TTDDialogTrigger.tsx, '{badge &&
{badge}
}' in DropdownMenuItemContent.tsx, and specific regex patterns for ordering. The instruction.md mentions the class name 'dropdown-menu-item__badge' and the general pattern but does not specify the exact JSX expressions like 'badge={AI}'

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction describes what needs to change (add badge prop, move badge from children to prop) but doesn't specify exact line numbers or exact code to write. It describes the desired behavior and pattern, not the exact implementation.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert exact string matches like 'badge={<DropdownMenu.Item.Badge>AI</DropdownMenu.Item.Badge>}' in test_actions_uses_badge_prop and test_ttd_dialog_trigger_uses_badge_prop, and exact whitespace-sensitive regex patterns in test_badge_rendered_in_correct_position. An alternative correct fix with different formatting (e.g., multiline badge prop, different indentation) would fail these tests.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The fail_to_pass tests (test_dropdown_menu_item_has_badge_prop, test_menu_item_content_has_badge_prop, test_actions_uses_badge_prop, test_font_picker_list_uses_badge_prop, test_ttd_dialog_trigger_uses_badge_prop, test_badge_rendered_in_correct_position) all use 'assert ... in open(path).read()' pattern - pure text matching on file contents with no code execution or behavioral testing.

test_not_tautological.reason: The fail_to_pass tests check for specific strings that don't exist in the base commit (e.g., 'badge?: React.ReactNode;' in DropdownMenuItem.tsx, 'badge={badge}' in the same file). A stub or no-op implementation would not add these strings to the files.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All behaviors described in instruction.md are tested: badge prop on DropdownMenuItem, badge prop on MenuItemContent, badge rendered in div with correct class, badge positioned after text before shortcut, and all three usage sites updated.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named (test_dropdown_menu_item_has_badge_prop, test_badge_rendered_in_correct_position, etc.), have descriptive docstrings indicating pass_to_pass vs fail_to_pass, and the module has an explanatory header comment.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task involves UI component refactoring, not structured data output.

no_typos.reason: File paths, component names, prop names, and class names are consistent between instruction.md and test files with no apparent typos.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: instruction.md names all 5 exact files to modify with full paths, specifies the exact prop name 'badge', the exact type 'React.ReactNode', and the exact CSS class 'dropdown-menu-item__badge'. This goes beyond describing what's broken and essentially dictates the implementation.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-only task modifying React components, not configuration files.
